explanation: Whose mercy encompasses all of creation without exception, in this life.
Who bestows special, lasting mercy upon the believers, especially in the Hereafter.
The absolute owner and ruler of all creation, with no partner in dominion.

meaning: The Most Sacred, The Most Holy
explanation: Utterly free from any imperfection, flaw, or resemblance to His creation.
Free from every defect, and the source of all safety and peace for His creation.

meaning: The Giver of Faith and Security
explanation: Who grants security to those who fear Him and confirms the truth of His promises.
Who watches over and safeguards all things, witnessing every affair.
The Overpowering One whom nothing can overcome or resist.
Who compels creation according to His will and mends what is broken.
Who is far above any flaw and free from any need of His creation, the truly Great.
Who brings all things into existence from nothing, exactly as He plans.
Who fashions creation free of any flaw, each thing distinct and well-proportioned.
Who gives every created thing its unique form and features.
Who repeatedly forgives the sins of His servants, again and again.
Who has total, unopposed power over all things and all creatures.
Who gives abundantly and freely, without seeking anything in return.
Who provides sustenance for every creature, believer and disbeliever alike.
Who opens the way to relief, guidance, and victory, and judges between His servants.
Whose knowledge encompasses everything, seen and unseen, without limit.
Who restricts provision, souls, or ease according to His wisdom.
Who expands provision, mercy, and ease for whomever He wills.
Who humbles the arrogant and lowers whom He wills in rank.
Who raises the status of whom He wills, in this life and the next.
Who grants honor and strength to whomever He wills.
Who brings low and disgraces whomever He wills, by His justice.
Who hears every sound and every silent thought, near or far.
Who sees all things, however hidden, with perfect clarity.
Whose judgment is final and perfectly just, in this life and the Hereafter.
Who is free of all injustice, whose every decree is perfectly fair.
Who is kind and gentle to His servants and aware of the finest details.
Who knows the inner reality of all things, not just their outward appearance.
Who delays punishment out of patience and mercy, giving sinners time to repent.
Whose greatness is beyond full human comprehension.
Whose forgiveness covers even the greatest of sins for those who turn to Him.
Who rewards good deeds abundantly, however small, and multiplies their reward.
Who is exalted above all creation in rank, power, and being.
Who is greater than everything that exists, in every sense.
Who protects His creation and preserves the deeds and records of every soul.
Who provides the strength and nourishment every creature needs to survive.
Who takes account of every deed with perfect precision, and suffices His servants.
Who possesses perfect majesty, greatness, and grandeur.
Who gives generously and honorably, far beyond what is asked or deserved.
Who watches over every soul and every deed at every moment.
Who answers the call of those who sincerely turn to Him in supplication.
Whose knowledge, mercy, and provision have no limit or boundary.
Whose every command and decree carries perfect wisdom and purpose.
Who loves His righteous servants and is beloved by the hearts of the believers.
Whose glory and generosity are perfect and complete.
Who will raise all creation to life again on the Day of Judgment.
Who witnesses every event and every deed, nothing escaping His sight.
Whose existence is absolute truth, and whose promise is always fulfilled.

meaning: The Trustee, The Disposer of Affairs
explanation: Who is sufficient for those who place their trust in Him, managing all affairs.
Whose strength is complete and never diminishes or tires.
Whose power is unshakeable, exerted with perfect ease.
Who is the true guardian and helper of the believers, near to those who love Him.
Who is deserving of all praise, in Himself and in all He does.
Who keeps perfect count of everything that exists, down to the smallest detail.
Who began the creation of all things from nothing, without any precedent.
Who will bring all creation back into being after death, as He first created it.
Who gives life to what was lifeless, in this world and at the Resurrection.
Who ordains the death of every living thing at its appointed time.
Whose life is perfect, everlasting, and without beginning or end.
Who sustains and maintains all of creation while needing nothing Himself.
Who lacks nothing and finds whatever He seeks with perfect ease.
Whose nobility and honor are complete, distinct from and beyond His glory alone.
Who is one without any partner, equal, or division in His being.
Whose oneness is absolute and unlike anything else, sharing His nature with none.
Whom all creation depends upon, while He depends upon nothing at all.
Who is capable of anything He wills, without any hindrance.
Whose power is total and decisive over every affair, great or small.
Who brings forward whom or whatever He wills, in rank or in time.
Who delays whom or whatever He wills, in rank or in time.
Who existed before all things, with nothing preceding Him.
Who remains after all creation ends, with nothing after Him.
Whose existence and signs are evident throughout all of creation.
Whose true essence is beyond the perception of any created being.
Who governs and manages the affairs of all creation.
Who is exalted far above any imperfection or comparison to creation.
Whose kindness and goodness toward His creation are abundant and unceasing.
Who repeatedly turns to His servants in forgiveness whenever they turn back to Him.
Who justly punishes the persistently wrongdoing after ample warning.
Who erases sins entirely, as though they had never happened.
Whose compassion is especially tender, sparing His servants from harm.
Who owns and rules all dominion, granting and removing power as He wills.
Who combines perfect majesty with perfect generosity toward His creation.
Who establishes justice and fairness in every judgment.
Who will gather all of creation together on the Day of Judgment.
Who needs nothing from His creation, while all creation needs Him.
Who grants sufficiency and independence to whomever He wills.
Who withholds harm from His creation and prevents what He wills.
Who permits harm to occur as part of His wisdom and justice, invoked alongside An-Nafi'.
Who is the true source of all benefit and good in creation.
Who is the light of the heavens and the earth, guiding hearts out of darkness.
Who guides whomever He wills to the straight path and to true understanding.
Who creates in ways without precedent or equal, unlike anything before.
Whose existence continues forever, without end.
Who remains after all creation perishes and inherits all that exists.

meaning: The Guide to the Right Path
explanation: Whose guidance leads directly to what is right, without any need for counsel.
Who is never hasty in punishing, giving His creation time and opportunity to repent.
